title: Design System: PROYA VOD Processing Dashboard

display.purpose: App title and rare identity-level headings.

title.purpose: Panel titles, KPI labels, module names, and card headings.

body.purpose: Tables, queue rows, captions, transcripts, metadata, and form labels.

label.purpose: Uppercase section kickers, compact badges, table headers, and mobile stat labels.

shadows.name: active-navigation-lift
shadows.purpose: Selected navigation or primary active context.

shadows.name: status-glow
shadows.purpose: Live or healthy status dots when paired with labels.

shadows.name: inset-track-line
shadows.purpose: Circular stage indicators and contained progress surfaces.

motion.name: state-standard
motion.purpose: Slightly larger reveal or panel feedback where Streamlit rerenders are stable.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Disable nonessential animation and keep state changes immediate.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Decisive action button for starting, continuing, applying, or confirming operator actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { min-height: 40px; padding: 10px 16px; border-radius: 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(96, 165, 250, 0.28); background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.95), rgba(29, 78, 216, 0.95)); color: #edf3ff; font: 700 0.92rem/1.2 Source Sans Pro, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: border-color 160ms ease-out, background 160ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { border-color: rgba(96, 165, 250, 0.42); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #93c5fd; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Quiet utility button for refresh, pagination, filters, and lower-risk controls.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { min-height: 40px; padding: 10px 16px; border-radius: 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.12); background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(19, 31, 50, 0.96), rgba(14, 22, 35, 0.96)); color: #edf3ff; font: 600 0.92rem/1.2 Source Sans Pro, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: border-color 160ms ease-out, color 160ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: rgba(96, 165, 250, 0.22); color: #f8fafc; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #93c5fd; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-secondary:disabled { color: #64748b; border-color: rgba(100, 116, 139, 0.16); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Mandatory status pattern: icon, text, color, and shape together.
components.html: Processing
components.css: .ds-status-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; padding: 5px 10px; border-radius: 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.12); font: 700 0.82rem/1 Source Sans Pro, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-status-badge svg { width: 14px; height: 14px; flex: 0 0 auto; } .ds-status-processing { color: #93c5fd; background: rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.16); border-color: rgba(59, 130, 246, 0.18); } .ds-status-badge:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #93c5fd; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Module Readiness Card
components.description: Compact readiness summary for hook, main, and CTA module inventory.
components.css: .ds-module-card { border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.12); border-radius: 8px; background: rgba(9, 15, 25, 0.28); color: #edf3ff; padding: 14px; min-width: 260px; font-family: Source Sans Pro, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-module-head { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 12px; margin-bottom: 14px; } .ds-module-head h3 { margin: 0; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.2; } .ds-module-head span { border-radius: 999px; padding: 4px 9px; color: #86efac; background: rgba(22, 163, 74, 0.14); border: 1px solid rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.18); font: 800 0.75rem/1 Source Sans Pro, system-ui, sans-serif; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-module-counts { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, minmax(0, 1fr)); gap: 9px; } .ds-module-counts div { border-radius: 8px; background: rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.58); padding: 9px 7px; } .ds-module-counts b { display: block; color: #edf3ff; font-size: 1.35rem; line-height: 1; } .ds-module-counts small { display: block; color: #94a3b8; font-size: 0.72rem; text-transform: uppercase; margin-top: 5px; }

components.name: Navigation Item
components.description: Desktop rail item with icon container, text label, and clear selected state.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { width: 100%; min-height: 44px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 14px; padding: 14px 16px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 12px; background: transparent; color: #d7e3f7; font: 600 0.92rem/1.2 Source Sans Pro, system-ui, sans-serif; text-align: left; } .ds-nav-icon { width: 28px; height: 28px; border-radius: 9px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.08); border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.08); color: #dbeafe; } .ds-nav-icon svg { width: 16px; height: 16px; } .ds-nav-item-active { background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(30, 64, 124, 0.92), rgba(25, 52, 102, 0.92)); border-color: rgba(96, 165, 250, 0.16); box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.22); } .ds-nav-item:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #93c5fd; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Dark Streamlit-compatible input surface for filters, paths, and search fields.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: 6px; color: #94a3b8; font: 700 0.82rem/1.2 Source Sans Pro, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-field input { min-height: 40px; border-radius: 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.14); background: rgba(12, 20, 33, 0.72); color: #edf3ff; padding: 10px 12px; font: 500 0.92rem/1.2 Source Sans Pro, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-field input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #93c5fd; outline-offset: 2px; border-color: rgba(96, 165, 250, 0.42); }

components.name: Progress Bar
components.description: Compact progress pattern with a numeric label. Bars never appear without text.
components.css: .ds-progress { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 11px; min-width: 160px; color: #dbeafe; font: 600 0.86rem/1.2 Source Sans Pro, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-progress-track { width: 102px; height: 10px; border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(30, 41, 59, 0.9); overflow: hidden; } .ds-progress-fill { height: 100%; border-radius: 999px; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #3b82f6, #60a5fa); }

components.name: Transcript Box
components.description: Readable transcript detail area with inline highlights for price and compliance issues.
components.html: Host explains the serum benefit. Rp99.000 needs review near claim text.
components.css: .ds-transcript { max-height: 220px; overflow-y: auto; border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.14); border-radius: 8px; padding: 13px; background: rgba(2, 8, 23, 0.22); color: #dbeafe; font: 400 0.86rem/1.55 Source Sans Pro, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-transcript mark { border-radius: 4px; padding: 1px 3px; } .ds-price { background: rgba(250, 204, 21, 0.28); color: #fef9c3; } .ds-violation { background: rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.30); color: #fee2e2; }

narrative.overview:
This system is a calm, status-first operations interface for office operators and editors running a high-throughput local video clipping pipeline. The physical scene is a remote PC or office workstation during long PROYA livestream processing runs: the operator needs to see queue health, failures, reviews, and ready assets without feeling pushed into a noisy debug console.

The dashboard is a product surface, so design serves the task. Familiar Streamlit controls are refined into a dark, legible, control-room vocabulary: dense tables, compact badges, restrained panels, and direct queue controls. The mood is composed, trustworthy, and efficient.

It explicitly rejects the anti-references from PRODUCT.md: flashy SaaS marketing patterns, toy-like controls, excessive decoration, decorative gradients, hero-scale type inside operational screens, dense technical log walls as the primary experience, and status colors that require memorization.
keyCharacteristics: Calm dark operational surfaces with clear tonal steps.
Status language that combines text, icon or shape, color, and hierarchy.
Compact tables for desktop review and record cards for mobile.
Restrained borders, limited shadows, and predictable controls.
Semantic color reserved for action, health, waiting, failure, and review.

rules.name: The Status Redundancy Rule
rules.body: Color never carries status alone. Every important state must include visible text plus icon, dot, progress shape, or badge shape.

rules.name: The Accent Budget Rule
rules.body: Blue, green, yellow, red, and violet are operational signals, not decoration. If a color does not explain state, selection, risk, or action, remove it.

rules.name: The Tinted Neutral Rule
rules.body: Avoid pure black and pure white in normal UI. Use the slate text and surface scale so long sessions remain readable.

rules.name: The Scan First Rule
rules.body: Numbers, status labels, and next actions get typographic priority over descriptive prose.

rules.name: The No Decorative Type Rule
rules.body: Do not introduce expressive display fonts, gradient text, or oversized hero typography inside this product UI.

rules.name: The Tonal Layer Rule
rules.body: Reach for a darker or lighter surface step and a border before adding a shadow.

rules.name: The Shadow Restraint Rule
rules.body: If every panel casts a shadow, no panel is important. Shadows are reserved for hierarchy and state.

dos: Do surface the next operational decision first: running, waiting, needs review, failed, blocked, ready, or completed.
Do use status text, icon or dot, color, and badge shape together every time status is a primary cue.
Do keep the palette restrained: cool slate neutrals carry the surface, semantic colors carry meaning.
Do keep desktop density useful with compact tables, short labels, and stable spacing.
Do swap dense desktop tables for mobile record cards below 760px.
Do keep controls predictable: standard buttons, tabs, select boxes, inputs, and pagination.
Do provide visible focus states for buttons, filters, tabs, dropdowns, table actions, and navigation.
Do support WCAG 2.2 AA, reduced-motion preferences, color-blind-safe status indicators, readable chart labels/tooltips, and mobile touch targets around 44px.
donts: Don't use flashy SaaS marketing patterns.
Don't use decorative gradients. Functional gradients are allowed only for surfaces, progress fills, and selected controls where they preserve readability.
Don't use hero-scale type inside operational screens.
Don't expose dense technical log walls as the primary experience.
Don't use status colors that require memorization.
Don't make the dashboard look like a generic analytics template, a developer debug panel, or a playful creator tool.
Don't add side-stripe borders thicker than 1px as accents on cards, rows, callouts, or alerts. Use full borders, background tints, icons, labels, or nothing.
Don't use gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, decorative motion, or display fonts in labels, buttons, tables, or data.
